channel.title: hybrid cloud on
channel.description: Recent content in hybrid cloud on

item.title: Google Distributed Cloud Edge
item.description: Overview Run Kubernetes clusters on-prem on dedicated hardware provided and maintained by Google Google engineers require physical access Deploy workloads like in GKE Provision new clusters in Cloud Console, CLI and API—similar to GKE Clusters can access VPC via loud VPN Use Cases Stable network connection required between Kubernetes workloads and on-prem Low latency to on-prem required Large data volumes—cost/performance prohibitive on GCP Regulatory/data sovereignty reasons Limitations Limited processing capacity Workload restrictions Anthos Service Mesh and Anthos Config Management not supported Architecture Rack of hardware—Distributed Cloud Edge Zone (GDC Edge Zone) Kubernetes control plane runs in GCP region GDC Edge Zone required constant Internet communication Remotely managed—e.

item.title: AWS Edge Computing
item.description: Why? Low latency Local data processing Data residency Use case: Online betting in the US—local regulations for data processing/storage locations AWS Hybrid Cloud Services Local Zones Outposts Wavelength How to Approach Questions to Ask Why can’t the workload go in region (i.

item.title: AWS Local Zones
item.description: Overview Subset of AWS compute infrastructure in Local Zones closer to metro areas Reduce latency for metros not near region

item.title: AWS Outposts
item.description: Overview Extend AWS infrastructure to on-prem Rack Fully-managed by AWS Available Functionality Compute ECS EKS EC2 Block and Object Storage S3 EBS EBS Local Snapshots Data RDS Elasticache EMR Networking Application Load Balancer (ALB) Outposts Server Use case: when full Outposts rack not required 1U and 2U servers Small workloads, e.

item.title: AWS Wavelength
item.description: Overview 5G Use cases: require sub 5ms latency
